scheme_name: PM POSHAN - Prime Minister's Overarching Scheme For Holistic Nourishment
page_title: PM POSHAN - Prime Minister's Overarching Scheme For Holistic Nourishment

details: PM POSHAN is a centrally sponsored scheme by the Department of School Education & Literacy, Ministry of Education. Under this scheme, one hot cooked meal will be provided to the children studying in Government and Government – aided schools. The Scheme is implemented across the country covering all the eligible children without any discrimination of gender and social class.

The main objectives of the PM POSHAN Abhiyaan are to address two of the pressing problems for the majority of children in India, viz. hunger and education by improving the nutritional status of eligible children in Government and Government-aided schools as well as encouraging poor children, belonging to disadvantaged sections, to attend school more regularly and help them concentrate on classroom activities.

POSHAN Abhiyaan is one of the many schemes under the umbrella scheme "Integrated Child Development Services Scheme (ICDS)". ICDS also includes Anganwadi Services Scheme, Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana, and Scheme for Adolescent Girls.
benefits: Nutrition norm per child per day:
Food norms per child per day:
Primary: Food grains - 100 gms; Pulses - 20 gms; Vegetables - 50 gms; Oil & fat - 5 gms, Salt & condiments - As per need.
Upper Primary: Food grains - 150 gms; Pulses - 30 gms; Vegetables - 75 gms; Oil & fat – 7.5 gms, Salt & condiments - As per need.

Under the Scheme, there is the provision of hot cooked meals to children of pre-schools or Bal Vatika (before class I) in primary schools also in addition to the 11.80 crore children of classes I to VIII studying in 11.20 lakh schools.
eligibility: The applicant must be a citizen of India.
The applicant must be studying in a Primary or Upper Primary class (Class 1st to Class 8th) in a Government or a Government-aided school.

offline: There is no application process for this scheme. PM POSHAN is equally applicable to all children who are studying in Government and Government – aided schools.

documents_required: No documents are required for availing the benefits of this scheme.

faqs.question: What is PM POSHAN Scheme?
faqs.answer: PM POSHAN is a centrally sponsored scheme by the Ministry of Education that provides hot cooked meals to children studying in Government and Government-aided schools to improve their nutritional status and encourage regular school attendance . PM POSHAN Ministry of Education regular school attendance

faqs.question: Who is eligible to benefit from PM POSHAN?
faqs.answer: All children studying in: Pre-school ( Bal Vatika ) within primary schools Class 1 to Class 8 in Government or Government-aided schools are eligible. There is no need to apply for the scheme. All children studying in: Pre-school ( Bal Vatika ) within primary schools Bal Vatika Class 1 to Class 8 Class 1 to Class 8

faqs.question: What kind of meals are provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides one hot cooked meal per school day that meets specific calorie and protein norms. The meal includes food grains, pulses, vegetables, oil/fat, and condiments. hot cooked meal grains, pulses, vegetables, oil/fat, and condiments.

faqs.question: What are the nutrition norms per child per day under PM POSHAN?
faqs.answer: Primary (Class 1–5): Calories: 450 kcal Protein: 12 grams Upper Primary (Class 6–8): Calories: 700 kcal Protein: 20 grams Primary (Class 1–5): Primary (Class 1–5): Calories: 450 kcal Protein: 12 grams Upper Primary (Class 6–8): Upper Primary (Class 6–8): Calories: 700 kcal Protein: 20 grams

faqs.question: What are the food quantity norms per child per day?
faqs.answer: Primary: Food grains: 100 g Pulses: 20 g Vegetables: 50 g Oil & fat: 5 g Upper Primary: Food grains: 150 g Pulses: 30 g Vegetables: 75 g Oil & fat: 7.5 g (Salt & condiments as per need) Primary: Primary: Food grains: 100 g Pulses: 20 g Vegetables: 50 g Oil & fat: 5 g Upper Primary: Upper Primary: Food grains: 150 g Pulses: 30 g Vegetables: 75 g Oil & fat: 7.5 g

faqs.question: Is there any discrimination based on caste, religion, or gender under PM POSHAN?
faqs.answer: No . The scheme is universal and inclusive , and benefits are provided to all eligible children without any discrimination. universal and inclusive all eligible children

faqs.question: Is there an application process for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No application is required. All eligible children automatically receive the benefits as part of their school program. No application All eligible children part of their school

faqs.question: What is the objective of PM POSHAN Scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to: Enhance nutrition for school children Encourage school attendance Improve concentration and learning outcomes Promote social equity through common meals The scheme aims to: Enhance nutrition for school children school children Encourage school attendance attendance Improve concentration and learning outcomes Promote social equity through common meals social equity

faqs.question: Is this scheme part of a broader initiative?
faqs.answer: Yes , PM POSHAN operates under the umbrella of the Integrated Child Development Services ( ICDS ), which includes: Anganwadi Services Scheme Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana Scheme for Adolescent Girls Yes ICDS Anganwadi Services Scheme Anganwadi Services Scheme Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana Scheme for Adolescent Girls Scheme for Adolescent Girls

faqs.question: How many children and schools are covered under PM POSHAN?
faqs.answer: The scheme benefits approximately more than 11.80 crore children across 11.20 lakh schools throughout India, making it one of the world’s largest school nutrition programs. 11.80 crore children 11.20 lakh schools
